decisions.title: 401 is the required response for all authentication failures
decisions.rule: Return HTTP 401 when: token is missing, token is invalid, token is expired, or token is malformed. No exceptions.
decisions.packaging_note: Must appear as a normative rule block in SKILL.md, not advisory prose.

decisions.title: 403 is strictly scoped to post-authentication permission failures
decisions.rule: Return HTTP 403 only when the caller is successfully authenticated but lacks permission for the requested resource. Never return 403 for token problems.
decisions.packaging_note: Must explicitly prohibit 403 for expired/malformed tokens — this is a common implementation error and must be called out.

decisions.title: WWW-Authenticate header is conditionally required with 401
decisions.rule: Include a WWW-Authenticate header in 401 responses when the authentication scheme permits or requires credential retry.
decisions.packaging_note: Must be framed as a conditional norm (when-then), not an absolute requirement.

decisions.title: Short-lived access tokens are recommended
decisions.rule: Prefer short-lived access tokens to reduce the exposure window for expired-token authentication failures.
decisions.packaging_note: Advisory weight only. Must not be presented with the same normative force as dec-01 or dec-02.

decisions.title: Operator-facing documentation must distinguish authentication from authorization
decisions.rule: Any operator-facing guidance (including this skill) must clearly distinguish authentication failure (401) from authorization failure (403) in its explanatory text.
decisions.packaging_note: This is a meta-obligation: it governs the skill content itself.

out_of_scope: Refresh token flows and 401 signalling when a refresh path is available
Token revocation and blacklisting response semantics
Rate-limiting (429) interaction with authentication retry loops
contradiction_summary: No contradictions in source set. One near-miss tension (expired/malformed 401 vs 403) fully resolved by src-02 explicit language.
